en.st_up_ret_b3_descr: A synthetic element installed onto rods ensures that they move strictly as intended, increasing accuracy.
en.st_up_ret_c1_descr: Thickening of the sleeve allows barrel pressure to be increased, improving bullet speed.
en.st_up_ret_c2_descr: A link that is opened by gases and closed by the return mechanism itself, which allows part of the gas energy to be used to operate the return mechanism.
en.st_up_ret_c3_descr: Replacement of the return mechanism with a more powerful version comprising two guiding rods and return springs. Increases the weapon's rate of fire.
en.st_up_ret_c3_name: Conversion to a two rod system
en.st_up_roun_a1_descr: Special gas exit canals around the drum part of the weapon that reduce recoil.
en.st_up_roun_a2_descr: Addition of diagonal cam slots onto the drum, which speed up rotation of the mechanism which uses gas energy.
en.st_up_roun_a2_name: Addition of diagonal cam slots onto the drum
en.st_up_run_1_descr: Installation of hydraulic boosters is a modification that is used for fourth generation exoskeletons. Allows for sprinting in an exoframe.
en.st_up_shock_1_descr: A thin layer of constantan is capable of isolating against electricity.
en.st_up_shock_2_descr: Constantan is capable of isolating against electricity and has a very high melting point.
en.st_up_shock_3_descr: This nickel and magnesium alloy is used in armour to prevent electric shocks.
en.st_up_shock_3_name: An isolation bodysuit with manganin inserts
en.st_up_stamina_1_descr: An ergonomic improvement: special mounts attached to the bodysuit allow for carried weight to feel lighter.
en.st_up_stamina_2_descr: An experimental model of a running system, which is not used outside the Zone due to its high price and dubious application.
en.st_up_stk_a1_descr: The advantage of this European standard modification is reduced weight without a fall in reliability.
en.st_up_stk_a2_descr: A high durability receiver helps assault rifles endure combat conditions on a daily basis.
en.st_up_stk_a3_descr: A simple addition of weight to the front of the receiver to reduce recoil.
en.st_up_stk_a3_name: Addition of counter-weight to the steel box
en.st_up_stk_b1_descr: Installation of jackets helps to address increases in the weapon's rate of fire, which produce additional heat that must be controlled.
en.st_up_stk_b2_descr: A more convenient handle shape, which allows for better handling of the weapon when firing on the move.
en.st_up_stk_c1_descr: A modification used exclusively for barrels with solid bullpup receivers, which simplifies maintenance of the weapon.
en.st_up_stk_c2_descr: This sputter prevents the barrel from overheating, removes manufacturing defects and significantly increases the barrel's life cycle.
en.st_up_stk_c2_name: Smoothing receiver groove and indent sputter
en.st_up_stk_c3_descr: A light mobile component is installed in the inclined handle to stabilize the weapon by creating a counter-effect to recoil when the weapon is fired.
en.st_up_stvd_a1_descr: The cavity inside the handle allows the weapon's weight to be reduced.
en.st_up_stvd_a2_descr: Lighter version of the standard stock.
en.st_up_stvd_a3_descr: A lighter poly-material allows the weapon's weight to be reduced.
en.st_up_stvd_b1_descr: Addition of a mass component to the weapon to reduce recoil.
en.st_up_stvd_b2_descr: A more modern and comfortable handle simplifies weapon handling.
en.st_up_stvd_b4_descr: A more modern and comfortable stock simplifies weapon handling.
en.st_up_stvd_b5_descr: A special coating protects the weapon from the elements.
en.st_up_stvd_c1_descr: A mercury recoil absorber is located in the weapon's stock, partially consuming some of the recoil when the weapon is fired.
en.st_up_stvd_c2_descr: A free-moving impulse counter-weight component, which moves from the bolt when the weapon is fired and absorbs some of the recoil.
en.st_up_stvh_a1_descr: A change of material reduces barrel damage from uneven heating during firing, which allows the weapon's rate of fire to be increased.
en.st_up_stvh_b1_descr: An important modification that protects the weapon from air temperature effects during firing.
en.st_up_stvh_b2_descr: A technological improvement, finning requires extreme precision to prevent the formation of overheating points on the barrel.
en.st_up_stvh_c1_descr: Replacement of the barrel with a more modern version allows for a considerable increase in accuracy.
en.st_up_stvp_a1_descr: An expensive frame for a weapon that will serve a long time.
en.st_up_stvp_a2_descr: In this case the frame is more reliable than composite.
en.st_up_stvp_b1_descr: A technical replacement of the pistol frame with a lighter one does not affect the weapon's reliability.
en.st_up_stvp_b2_descr: A more modern and comfortable handle simplifies weapon handling.
en.st_up_stvp_b4_descr: Protective thickening flaps should protect the weapon from dirt and impact.
en.st_up_stvp_b5_descr: A change in the rear section makes for a more ergonomic handle shape.
en.st_up_stvp_b5_name: Changing handle shape with a straight rear section
en.st_up_stvp_c1_descr: This modification is only possible if a return spring has been installed into the handle.
en.st_up_stvp_c1_name: Installation of a return spring amortization component
en.st_up_stvp_c2_descr: The possibility of choosing a suitable plate makes for a more ergonomic handle shape.
en.st_up_stvp_c2_name: A handle with a changed shape with replaceable plates
en.st_up_stvs_a1_descr: Intended for weapons that are used often, the jacket protects them from early wear.
en.st_up_stvs_a1_name: Installation of a perforated steel jacket
en.st_up_stvs_b1_descr: A more technological and more recoil dampening component than used in regular automatic weapons.
en.st_up_stvs_b2_descr: A more modern and suitable barrel for sniper fire at very long range.
en.st_up_stvs_b2_name: Installation of a heavy barrel section with polygon rifling
en.st_up_stvs_b3_descr: Longitudinal fullers of the barrel section prevent the barrel from overheating and dirt contamination.
en.st_up_stvs_b3_name: Longitudinal fullers of the barrel section
en.st_up_stvs_c2_descr: Sinusoidal rifling of the barrel chamber is usually performed on assault weapons to increase their rate of fire.
en.st_up_tele_1_descr: The closed band has been demonstrated to provide partial protection against direct psy-emissions.
en.st_up_tele_1_name: A psy-protection band made of paired steel grills
en.st_up_usm_a1_descr: The purpose of the bolt hammer compensator is to absorb some of the recoil, improving groupings when firing in bursts.
en.st_up_usm_a2_descr: One of the simplest and most popular modifications, which allows trigger resistance to be reduced. Simple and handy.
en.st_up_usm_b1_descr: A special selection of components made from various materials specific to that weapon. It may not be cheap, but it increases the life cycle of your firearm.
en.st_up_usm_b2_descr: Allows the rate of fire to be increased at the cost of accuracy and reliability.
en.st_up_usm_b3_descr: Removal of the combat spring allows the first shot to be made half a second earlier. It may not be much, but it can save your life.
en.st_up_usm_c1_descr: Protective sputter makes misfiring due to firing mechanism malfunction impossible.
en.st_up_usm_c2_descr: Boltless fixing allows for automatic load to be increased, considerably improving the weapon's rate of fire.
en.st_up_usm_c2_name: Boltless fixing of the firing mechanism
en.st_up_usm_c3_descr: The impactless system pierces the round instead of striking it by means of a special hammer, which makes the firing process considerably softer.
en.st_up_usm_c3_name: Installation of an impactless firing mechanism system
en.st_up_usmp_a1_descr: The trigger is armed by the first shot, with all other shots occurring during automatic trigger arming.
en.st_up_usmp_b1_descr: One of the simplest and most popular modifications, which allows for trigger resistance to be reduced. Simple and handy.
en.st_up_usmp_b2_descr: Partial arming allows firing accuracy to be improved.
en.st_up_usmp_b3_descr: Allows the same pressure to be applied to the trigger from the first to the last shot, which has a positive effect on ease of use.
en.st_up_usmp_b4_descr: A modern modification which prevents the weapon from bolt section detachment.
en.st_up_usmp_b5_descr: A modular firing mechanism provides easier maintenance and parts replacement, as well as certain improvements in ease of use.
en.st_up_usmp_c1_descr: The arming component considerably reduces the amount of pressure required on the trigger, improving accuracy.
en.st_up_usmp_c2_descr: A new technological development that allows a simple adjustment to change the firing mechanism mode - another step towards better ergonomics and easy weapon adjustment.
en.st_up_usmp_c3_descr: Once the first shot is fired, half the trigger movement is required to make subsequent shots.
en.st_up_weight_1_descr: Replacing metal with carbon fibre allows the weight of body armour to be reduced without sacrificing reliability.
en.st_up_weight_2_descr: A lighter aluminium frame for plates to replace the steel frame. Reduces armour weight
en.st_up_weight_3_descr: Use of lighter materials to reduce body armour weight.
en.st_up_weight_3_name: Installation of lighter synthetic and plastic components
en.st_up_weight_4_descr: Fabric, clips and thickeners are replaced with lighter synthetic materials to reduce armour weight.
en.st_up_zat_a1_descr: This fairly expensive modification is actually common for new generation weapons.
en.st_up_zat_a2_descr: Thickening of the bolt section prevents energy dissipation, which improves flatness.
en.st_up_zat_a2_name: Bolt section thickening with synthetic inserts
en.st_up_zat_a3_descr: Despite the name, it is the bolt that is replaced, as it has to endure the consequences of an improved rate of fire.
en.st_up_zat_b1_descr: Gas vent comes with a block that moves at the same speed, taking on part of the recoil.
en.st_up_zat_b1_name: Installation of a gas vent with a moving block
en.st_up_zat_b2_descr: A second piston moves the counter-weight, compensating for the bolt mechanism movement impulse.
en.st_up_zat_b2_name: Installation of an additional gas piston
en.st_up_zat_b3_descr: Protective sputter increases reliability and, more importantly, ensures airtightness, which prevents excessive energy dissipation.
en.st_up_zat_b4_descr: The purpose of titled grooves on the receiver is to reduce friction and catch small particles, preventing contamination of the bolt section.
en.st_up_zat_b5_descr: Reducing the gas chamber diameter by means of synthetic thickeners considerably increases bullet speed.
en.st_up_zat_b6_descr: On its way back to its starting position, the bolt redirects the air flow via the barrel chamber, cooling it.
en.st_up_zat_c1_descr: The weapon's bolt slides along the barrel, preventing upward movement of the muzzle section.
en.st_up_zat_c2_descr: Installation of a gas pistol stops gases from entering the bolt frame, which considerably reduces weapon dirtiness.
en.st_up_zat_c3_descr: This system collects some of the gases using the bolt section to improve its movement.
en.st_up_zat_c4_descr: A replacement of the automatic parts is more of an optimization than improvement of the gas piston. Provides a considerable increase in the weapon's rate of fire.
en.st_up_zat_c5_descr: Often used in modern weaponry, a roller locking system reduces recoil and improves groupings.
en.st_up_zat_c5_name: Change to a roller system with a semifree bolt
en.st_up_zath_a1_descr: In absorbing some of the heat, the components reduce weapon temperature changes during firing.
en.st_up_zath_b1_descr: A longer movement of the gas piston slightly increases weapon length, with a serious improvement in flatness.
en.st_up_zath_b2_descr: A moving parts buffer evenly distributes recoil throughout the weapon when it is fired, making it less pronounced.
en.st_up_zath_b2_name: Installation of a special moving parts buffer
en.st_up_zath_c1_descr: Symmetrical force cooling of a part of the barrel happens due to the work of an ejector and prevents weapon overheating.
en.st_up_zatp_a1_descr: Provides an increase in rate of fire without overloading other parts through additional recoil.
en.st_up_zatp_b1_descr: Provides an increase in flatness thanks to improved transfer of impulse to the bullet.
en.st_up_zatp_b2_descr: Softens recoil by slowing down bolt section movement.
en.st_up_zatp_b3_descr: Sputter protects weapons from early wear.
en.st_up_zatp_b4_descr: Improves flatness thanks to improved transfer of impulse to the bullet.
en.st_up_zatp_b5_descr: A heavier and closed bolt prevents the pistol's bolt section from malfunctioning.
en.st_up_zatp_b6_descr: A small buffer that takes on some of the recoil.
en.st_up_zatp_b7_descr: Installation of a steel insert helps to reduce recoil.
en.st_up_zatp_b7_name: Installation of a closing insert with a shaped spiral groove
en.st_up_zatp_b8_descr: By increasing bolt movement and reinforcing the return mechanism appropriately, you can improve flatness.
en.st_up_zatp_c1_descr: Short movement considerably increases the weapon's rate of fire without sacrificing flatness.
en.st_up_zatp_c1_name: Installation of a short bolt movement arrangement
en.st_up_zatp_c2_descr: Long bolt movement gives a bullet greater energy.
en.st_up_zatp_c2_name: Installation of a long barrel movement arrangement
en.st_up_zatp_c3_descr: A replacement of the automatic parts is more of an optimization than improvement of the gas piston. This considerably improves the weapon's rate of fire.
en.st_up_zatp_c3_name: Installation of a short movement recoil energy use arrangement
en.st_up_zatp_c4_descr: Installation of this module considerably improves groupings without sacrificing firing speed.
en.st_up_zatp_c5_descr: Introduction of additional compensation holes brings about a significant reduction in recoil.
en.st_up_zatp_c6_descr: Modernization of the pistol's automatic parts suitable for automatic fire.
en.st_up_zatp_c7_descr: Improvement of the pistol's automatic parts suitable for three round burst fire.
en.st_upgr_o_rad: Active filters and radiation shielding materials
en.st_vodka2_descr:
Following the tradition of naming vodka brands after Russian leaders, Putinka first went into production after Vladimir Putin was elected president. Produced in Russia from the finest grain and crystal clear water from Siberian springs. Triple purification, smooth texture, unique taste are not the only characteristics of this beverage. Drink with moderation.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  drink
  •  stimulant
  •  strong alcohol
en.st_vodka_descr:
Nemiroff is a Ukrainian producer of alcoholic beverages, specialising in vodkas and other spirits, based in the town of Nemyriv in the Vinnytska oblast. One of the best vodkas in the world and - by extension - in the Zone.
 
 NOTE:
 
 It is commonly known that this brand of alcohol is preferred by the Ecologist's chef at Yantar. Rumours exist that he'll happily smuggle military gear into the hands of common stalkers in exchange for a few swigs from a bottle, but such stories have yet to be substantiated.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  drink
  •  stimulant
  •  strong alcohol
en.st_vodka_quality_descr:
A favourite amongst Russians and many people the world over. A Russian vodka of high quality. Highly popular in the Zone and widely available most probably owing to an outside connection of one of the traders. In order to avoid detection by the military units stationing around the Zone, it is usually shipped in army containers or metal barrels, and then distributed in sturdy metal bottles.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  stimulant
  •  strong alcohol
  •  high quality
en.st_water_drink_descr:
Sparkling water for thirsty stalkers. It has some exhilarating effect when you're tired.
 
 PROPERTIES:
  •  drink
  •  imported
  •  mild alcohol sobering effect
  •  clean nutrition source
en.st_wpn_9a91_descr:
The 9A91 is a gas operated, rotating bolt weapon, which utilises a long stroke gas piston, located above the barrel, and a rotating bolt with 4 lugs. The receiver is made from steel stampings, the forend and pistol grip are made from polymer. The steel stock folds up and above the receiver when not in use. The flip up rear sight has settings for 100 and 200 metres range, but the relatively short sight base and steep trajectory of the subsonic bullet restricts the 9A-91 to ranges of about 100 metres.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO, PSO-1M2-1 scopes
  •  9A-91/VSK-94 suppressor
en.st_wpn_abakan_descr:
A key improvement of the AN-94 over the AK-74 is the introduction of the additional 2-rounds burst mode. Nikonov design delays the force of recoil until the fired rounds have left the barrel, which along with a unique 1800 rpm two-shot burst function increases body armour piercing capabilities of this firearm. Used by Spetsnaz operators as well as Duty members to take out armoured targets at relatively long ranges.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
en.st_wpn_abakan_n_descr:
A key improvement of the AN-94 over the AK-74 is the introduction of the additional 2-rounds burst mode. Nikonov design delays the force of recoil until the fired rounds have left the barrel, which along with a unique 1800 rpm two-shot burst function increases body armour piercing capabilities of this firearm. Used by Spetsnaz operators as well as Duty members to take out armoured targets at relatively long ranges.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  Scopes: All dovetail mount scopes except of PSO scopes with eye cup due to the inconvenience of using these scopes on AK type of rifles and 1P59 Giperon.
en.st_wpn_ace21_descr:
The Galil ACE family is designed and produced by the Israeli arms company IWI Ltd. The basis of the ACE Galil rifles is the Galil assault rifle, which in its turn is based on Kalashnikov's AK rifle. The Galil ACE focuses on ergonomics, ease of use and integration with additional attachments, as well as flexibility in combat - the Galil ACE family includes the three most common types of ammunition in the world (5.56x45 NATO, 7.62x39 M43 and NATO 7.62x51), with multiple barrel lengths for each type. This particular variant utilises 5.56x45 NATO rounds and has a compact barrel. In the Zone, this assault rifle is a real rarity.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_adar2_15_descr:
The ADAR 2-15 Russian civilian carbine that is based on the design of the AR-15. The carbine is produced in St. Petersburg with use of Israeli components and chrome barrels made by Molot Arms.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight, 1P76 Rakurs (low mount) and OKP-7.
  •  Suppressor: ADVANCED ARMAMENT (AAC) M4-2000
en.st_wpn_aek971_camo_descr:
Based on previous AK rifles in internal design and layout, the AEK-971 is also equipped with a recoil-balancing mechanism, resulting in more controllable automatic fire. Small batches of this rifle were manufactured for the Russian MVD. This model specifically has a light-beige colour with red stains, symbolizing the Duty faction. Some mechanisms were reassembled or replaced with more reliable ones. Parts are fitted tighter which results in higher muzzle speed.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  TGP-A suppressor
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_aek971_descr:
Based on previous AK rifles in internal design and layout, the AEK-971 is also equipped with a recoil-balancing mechanism, resulting in more controllable automatic fire. Small batches of this rifle were manufactured for the Russian MVD.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  TGP-A suppressor
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_aek973_descr:
The AEK-973 is a 7.62x39mm version of AEK-971 developed at the Kovrov Mechanical Plant under the direction of Stanislav Ivanovich Koksharov based on SA-006 system. Based on previous AK rifles in internal design and layout, the AEK-973 is also equipped with a recoil-balancing mechanism, resulting in more controllable automatic fire. Small batches of this rifle were manufactured for the Russian MVD.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  TGP-A suppressor
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher
en.st_wpn_ak101_descr:
The AK-101 is an assault rifle of the Kalashnikov family designed for the export market, using 5.56x45mm NATO cartridges; the standard of most NATO countries. The AK-101 is marketed at those looking for a weapon that combines the logistical compatibility and familiarity of the 5.56x45mm NATO round with the reliability of a Kalashnikov. It is designed with modern and composite materials, including plastics to reduce weight and to improve accuracy. Many of the improvements found in the AK-101 are also present in the AK-103 and the rest of the AK-10x series of rifles.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech, EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  ATG suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak102_descr:
The AK-102 is a 5.56x45mm carbine version of the AK-101 rifle. It features a shortened barrel that makes it a middle ground between a full rifle and the more compact AKS-74U. The rifle's receiver is made of stamped steel; magazine is made out of reinforced fibreglass and the stock is made of hollow plastic; making it lighter, more durable, and allowing a field kit to be stored inside of it.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech, EKP-8-02 Kobra, PSO-1 scopes
  •  ATG suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak103_descr:
The AK-103 assault rifle is a derivative of the AK-74M chambered for 7.62x39mm, same as the AKM and AK-47. The AK-103 can be fitted with a variety of sights including: laser, night vision, and telescopic sights, plus a suppressor and the GP-25 grenade launcher. It uses plastic components where ever possible instead of wood or metal to reduce weight.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  ATG suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak104_alfa_descr:
Customized version of the 7.62 mm AK-104 assault rifle. Looks like a finger tip of some Mid-Eastern operators...
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  All scopes for picatinny/weaver
  •  Silencer: 5.45/7.62x39 Hexagon Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak104_descr:
The AK-104 is a 7.62x39mm carbine version of the AK-103 rifle. It features a shortened barrel that makes it a middle ground between a full rifle and the more compact AKS-74U. The rifle's receiver is made of stamped steel; the magazine is made out of reinforced fibreglass and the stock is made of hollow plastic, making it lighter, more durable, and allowing a field kit to be stored inside of it.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  ATG suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak105_bas_descr:
The AK-105 is a 5.45x39mm carbine version of the AK-74M rifle. The AK-105 is supplementing AKS-74U carbines in Russian Army service. Ideal for CQC (close quarters combat), it's not intended for long range combat. The rifle's reiver is made of stamped steel; the magazine is made out of reinforced fibreglass and the stock is made of hollow plastic, making it lighter, more durable, and allowing a field kit to be stored inside of it.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: all scopes for Dovetail mount except of PSO scopes with eye cup due to the inconvenience of using these scopes on AK type of rifles and 1P59 Giperon.
  •  Can be upgraded using Tactical Kit.
  •  Silencer: TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak105_bas_shakal_descr:
The AK-105 is a 5.45x39mm carbine version of the AK-74M rifle. The AK-105 is supplementing AKS-74U carbines in Russian Army service. A very rare variant of the AK-105. It is found mainly among the veterans of the Duty group. Due to the presence of several sighting devices, it is ideal for combat both at close and medium-long distances.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The weapon was upgraded using "Shakal" Tactical Kit
  •  Scopes: Trijicon Acog, Trijicon RMR and AN/PEQ-15 LAM (integrated)
  •  Silencer: 5.45/7.62x39 Hexagon Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak105_bas_swamp_descr:
The AK-105 is a 5.45x39mm carbine version of the AK-74M rifle. The AK-105 is supplementing AKS-74U carbines in Russian Army service. A very rare variant of the AK-105. It is found mainly among the veterans of the Clear Sky group. Due to the presence of several sighting devices, it is ideal for combat both at close and medium-long distances.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The weapon was upgraded using "Swamp Thing" Tactical Kit
  •  Scopes: Elcan SpecterDr, Trijicon RMR and AN/PEQ-15 LAM (integrated)
  •  Silencer: 5.45/7.62x39 Hexagon Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak105_descr:
The AK-105 is a 5.45x39mm carbine version of the AK-74M rifle. The AK-105 is supplementing AKS-74U carbines in Russian Army service. Ideal for CQC (close quarters combat), it's not intended for long range combat. The rifle's reiver is made of stamped steel; the magazine is made out of reinforced fibreglass and the stock is made of hollow plastic, making it lighter, more durable, and allowing a field kit to be stored inside of it.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP 1S-03 "Kobra", 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak105_sp_descr:
The modification was based on the usual AK-105, which, as a result, (thanks to mercenaries craftsmen) for a long time turned into a favorite burrel of these guys.A very balanced weapon with low recoil, good accuracy and high wear resistance.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Leupold Deltapoint Pro Red Dot sight preinstalled on NcStar 45 mount
  •  Scopes: All scopes for dowetail mount except of PSO scopes with an eyecup, 1P59 Giperon and red dot sights.
  •  Silencer: TGP-A.
en.st_wpn_ak12_bas_descr:
The Kalashnikov AK-12 assault rifle is the newest creation of the IZMASH factory. It is intended to replace older Kalashnikov AK-74M and AK-100 series rifles for domestic use, as well as for export. The rifle is a gas operated, selective fire rifle using traditional "Kalashnikov type" action with long stroke gas piston and rotary bolt locking. The bolt group is noticeably lightened to decrease recoil and improve accuracy in automatic fire.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  All scopes for picatinny/weaver
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher
  •  5.45/7.62x39 Hexagon Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak12_custom_descr:
A rare stuff. Modified version of AK-12 2012. Mainly used by the Monolithh fuction. Has several modes of firing: automatic, single and, three rounds.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The weapon can be upgraded using tactical kit
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny
  •  Silencer: 5.45/7.62 Hexagon Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak12_descr:
The Kalashnikov AK-12 assault rifle is the newest creation of the IZMASH factory. It is intended to replace older Kalashnikov AK-74M and AK-100 series rifles for domestic use, as well as for export. The rifle is a gas operated, selective fire rifle using traditional "Kalashnikov type" action with long stroke gas piston and rotary bolt locking. The bolt group is noticeably lightened to decrease recoil and improve accuracy in automatic fire.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher
  •  TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak12_m1_descr:
The version of the AK-12 presented to the public in 2016. It differs quite significantly in appearance and in the design of a number of important units from the previous version of the AK-12 shown in 2015. The design of the machine was revised both to eliminate a number of identified shortcomings and problems, and in the direction of improving manufacturability, taking into account the wishes of the military.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher
  •  Silencer: 5.45/7.62 Hexagon Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak12_mono_com_descr:
A rare stuff. Modified version of AK-12 2012. Mainly used by the Monolith squad commanders fuction. Has several modes of firing: automatic, single and three rounds.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny
  •  Silencer: 5.45/7.62 Hexagon Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak12_mono_kit_descr:
A rare stuff. Modified version of AK-12 Monolith. Mainly used by the Monolithh fuction. Has several modes of firing: automatic, single and, three rounds. Thanks to the new trigger group, it is a more advanced and powerful weapon than the original. Increased rate of fire and improved balancing. This is irreversible upgrade.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The weapon was upgraded using "Monolith" Tactical Kit
  •  Scopes: Trijicon Acog and AN/PEQ-16 LAM (integrated).
  •  "Guard" Grenade Launcher.
  •  Silencer: Hexagon 5.45/7.62 Sound Suppressor.
en.st_wpn_ak5c_bas_5c_tik_descr:
The Ak 5 or Automatkarbin 5 ("automatic carbine 5", "automatic carbine" being the Swedish term for assault rifle) is the Swedish version of the FN FNC assault rifle with certain modifications, mostly to adapt the weapon to the partially subarctic Swedish climate. The Ak 5 is the service rifle of the Swedish Armed Forces. The Bofors Ak 5C is the modernized version of the original Ak 5, following the trend of modular weapons. One of the most significant improvements is the MIL-STD-1913 rail system to which a variety of different optics, lights and sights can be mounted, such as telescopic sights and image intensifiers. The double gas position, iron sights and bolt catch of the original Ak 5 rifle family were discarded.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The weapon was upgraded with tactical kit. The parts in the kit: low profile foregrip, LA5 LAM, Leupold LCO+D'evo scopes and short barrel. (Irreversible upgrade).
  •  Scopes installed: Leupold (LCO), Leupold D'evo x6 and LA5 Laser Aim Module.
  •  Suppressor: Rifle Dynamics ISG Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak5c_bas_descr:
The Ak 5 or Automatkarbin 5 ("automatic carbine 5", "automatic carbine" being the Swedish term for assault rifle) is the Swedish version of the FN FNC assault rifle with certain modifications, mostly to adapt the weapon to the partially subarctic Swedish climate. The Ak 5 is the service rifle of the Swedish Armed Forces. The Bofors Ak 5C is the modernized version of the original Ak 5, following the trend of modular weapons. One of the most significant improvements is the MIL-STD-1913 rail system to which a variety of different optics, lights and sights can be mounted, such as telescopic sights and image intensifiers. The double gas position, iron sights and bolt catch of the original Ak 5 rifle family were discarded.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Can be upgraded using Tactical Kit.
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver.
  •  Suppressor: NATO
en.st_wpn_ak5c_isg_descr:
The ISG modification of Swedish AK5C. Rare weapon in Zone. Rarely used by some ISG squads. The Ak 5 or Automatkarbin 5 ("automatic carbine 5", "automatic carbine" being the Swedish term for assault rifle) is the Swedish version of the FN FNC assault rifle with certain modifications, mostly to adapt the weapon to the partially subarctic Swedish climate. The Ak 5 is the service rifle of the Swedish Armed Forces. The Bofors Ak 5C is the modernized version of the original Ak 5, following the trend of modular weapons. One of the most significant improvements is the MIL-STD-1913 rail system to which a variety of different optics, lights and sights can be mounted, such as telescopic sights and image intensifiers. The double gas position, iron sights and bolt catch of the original Ak 5 rifle family were discarded.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver. LA5 LAM (installed).
  •  Suppressor: Rifle Dynamics ISG Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74_descr:
The AK-74 is a 5.45x45mm assault rifle developed in the early 1970s by Mikhail Kalashnikov to replace the earlier AKM. It was introduced in 1974 and first saw service with Soviet forces engaged in the Afghanistan Conflict. The AKM rifle was adapted to fire smaller 5.45x45mm cartridge and fitted with a large muzzle brake.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74_eot_descr:
The AK-74 is a 5.45x45mm assault rifle developed in the early 1970s by Mikhail Kalashnikov to replace the earlier AKM. It was introduced in 1974 and first saw service with Soviet forces engaged in the Afghanistan Conflict. The AKM rifle was adapted to fire smaller 5.45x45mm cartridge and fitted with a large muzzle brake. This particular model has an integrated EOTech holosight and a 45 round magazine. 
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  EOTech sight (integrated)
  •  TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74_isg_descr:
An advanced version of the AK-74M developed by the American company Rifle Dynamics. It is one of the most high-tech weapons in the Zone. Used by ISG special units.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ATPIAL (Advanced Target Pointer Illuminator Aiming Laser) AN/PEQ-15 produced by L3 Insight Technologies. Tactical device that combines laser designators in both visible and IR band with IR searchlight. 
  •  Universal mount by NcStar with Trijicon RMR red dot sight installed on it. 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver.
  •  Silencer: Rifle Dynamics ISG Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74_n_descr:
The AK-74 is a 5.45x45mm assault rifle developed in the early 1970s by Mikhail Kalashnikov to replace the earlier AKM. It was introduced in 1974 and first saw service with Soviet forces engaged in the Afghanistan Conflict. The AKM rifle was adapted to fire smaller 5.45x45mm cartridge and fitted with a large muzzle brake.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  All dovetail mount scopes except of PSO scopes with eye cup due to the inconvenience of using these scopes on AK type of rifles and 1P59 Giperon. 
  •  ATG suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74_old_descr:
The AK-74 is a 5.45x45mm assault rifle developed in the early 1970s by Mikhail Kalashnikov to replace the earlier AKM. It was introduced in 1974 and first saw service with Soviet forces engaged in the Afghanistan Conflict. The AKM rifle was adapted to fire smaller 5.45x45mm cartridge and fitted with a large muzzle brake. This particular variant was assembled a long time ago.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
 •  None due to the absence of a dovetail mount
  •  TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74_pmc_descr:
A modification of the Kalashnikov assault rifle, developed in 1970 for the 5.45x39 cartridge. It differs from the standard version by the installed body kit, which includes a tactical grip, a forend with picatinny rails and a folding telescopic buttstock, which significantly improves ergonomics of the gun. The kit also includes a laser targeting device, a tactical flashlight and an adapter mount for sighting devices. In the Zone similar models are usually used by mercenaries, Ukrainian special forces, various PMCs and other power structures for conducting special operations.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ATPIAL (Advanced Target Pointer Illuminator Aiming Laser) AN/PEQ-15 produced by L3 Insight Technologies. Tactical device that combines laser designators in both visible and IR band with IR searchlight. 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver and for Dovetail mount except of PSO scopes with eyecup, EOTS-62 and 1P59 Giperon. 
  •  Silencer: ATG
en.st_wpn_ak74m_beard_descr:
This AK, a kind of beard venom (not to be confused with his tea). Lightweight and tailored to the harsh conditions of the Zone. Does not provide for mounting a grenade launcher. It is made at Skadovsk and Yanov Station, from where it disperses throughout the Zone.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: all scopes for Dovetail mount (except of 1p59 Giperon and PSO scopes with an eyecup) and all red dot sights (except of Aimpoint PRO and Aimpoint T1 with high mount and Barska 11090). The use of PSO scopes with an eyecup on this weapon is highly not recomended.
  •  Silencer: TGP-A. No Grenade Launcher available for this model
en.st_wpn_ak74m_descr:
In 1991, the Izhmash factory began production of an improved AK-74 - the AK-74M assault rifle. Apart from several minor production improvements, the rifle also features a new foldable synthetic stock made from a black, glass-filled polyamide. The AK-74M also uses a reinforced muzzle device and dust cover. Each AK-74M is fitted with a side-rail bracket for mounting optics. The AK-74M has been accepted as the new service rifle of the Russian Federation.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74m_n_descr:
In 1991, the Izhmash factory began production of an improved AK-74 - the AK-74M assault rifle. Apart from several minor production improvements, the rifle also features a new foldable synthetic stock made from a black, glass-filled polyamide. The AK-74M also uses a reinforced muzzle device and dust cover. Each AK-74M is fitted with a side-rail bracket for mounting optics. The AK-74M has been accepted as the new service rifle of the Russian Federation
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  All dovetail mount scopes except of PSO scopes with eye cup due to the inconvenience of using these scopes on AK type of rifles and 1P59 Giperon.
  •  Silencer: TGP-A Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74m_pk-a_descr:
In 1991, the Izhmash factory began production of an improved AK-74 - the AK-74M assault rifle. Apart from several minor production improvements, the rifle also features a new foldable synthetic stock made from a black, glass-filled polyamide. The AK-74M also uses a reinforced muzzle device and dust cover. Each AK-74M is fitted with a side-rail bracket for mounting optics. The AK-74M has been accepted as the new service rifle of the Russian Federation. This model has a PK-A sight installed on the MTK-83 optical mount.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  PK-A red dot sight (integrated)
  •  TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74u_descr:
The AKS-74U was intended for use mainly by Spetsnaz units, airborne infantry, rear-echelon support units and armoured vehicle crews, who require a compact but relatively powerful individual automatic weapon. The AKS-74U is significantly more manoeuvrable in tight quarters than the AKS-74, but a significant decline in muzzle velocity resulted in a decrease in effective range.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  PBS-4 suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74u_isg_descr:
A custom model developed on the basis of AKSU by the American company Rifle Dynamics. It is one of the most high-tech weapons in the Zone. Used by ISG special units.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver
  •  Silencer: Rifle Dynamics ISG Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74u_m1_isg_descr:
Modification RD-74U ISG, developed by the American company Rifle Dynamics. It is one of the most high-tech weapons in the Zone. Used by ISG special units.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ATPIAL (Advanced Target Pointer Illuminator Aiming Laser) AN/PEQ-15 produced by L3 Insight Technologies. Tactical device that combines laser designators in both visible and IR band with IR searchlight. 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver
  •  Silencer: Rifle Dynamics ISG Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74u_n1_descr:
The AKS-74U was intended for use mainly by Spetsnaz units, airborne infantry, rear-echelon support units and armoured vehicle crews, who require a compact but relatively powerful individual automatic weapon. The AKS-74U is significantly more manoeuvrable in tight quarters than the AKS-74, but a significant decline in muzzle velocity resulted in a decrease in effective range.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for dovetail mount (except of PSO scopes with eye cup and 1P59 Giperon). The use of PSO scopes with an eyecup on this weapon is highly not recomended.
  •  Silencer: PBS-4 suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74u_old_descr:
The AKS-74U was intended for use mainly by Spetsnaz units, airborne infantry, rear-echelon support units and armoured vehicle crews, who require a compact but relatively powerful individual automatic weapon. The AKS-74U is significantly more manoeuvrable in tight quarters than the AKS-74, but a significant decline in muzzle velocity resulted in a decrease in effective range. This model seems to be very old.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
 •  None due to the absence of a dovetail mount
  •  PBS-4 suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak74u_snag_descr:
The AKS-74U was intended for use mainly by Spetsnaz units, airborne infantry, rear-echelon support units and armoured vehicle crews, who require a compact but relatively powerful individual automatic weapon. The AKS-74U is significantly more manoeuvrable in tight quarters than the AKS-74, but a significant decline in muzzle velocity resulted in a decrease in effective range. This special variant has a 45 round RPK magazine, an integrated Kobra sight and an integrated suppressor.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-1S-03 Kobra sight (integrated)
  •  PBS-4 suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_ak74u_tac_descr:
The AKS-74U was intended for use mainly by Spetsnaz units, airborne infantry, rear-echelon support units and armoured vehicle crews, who require a compact but relatively powerful individual automatic weapon. The AKS-74U is significantly more manoeuvrable in tight quarters than the AKS-74, but a significant decline in muzzle velocity resulted in a decrease in effective range. This special variant has a 45 round RPK magazine.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for dovetail mount (except of PSO scopes with eye cup and 1P59 Giperon) and all Red Dot Sights except of Aimpoint PRO and Aimpoint T1 with high mount.
  •  Silencer: PBS-4 suppressor
en.st_wpn_ak_descr:
The AK-47 can be described as a hybrid of previous rifle technology. Mikhail Kalashnikov designed this automatic rifle combining the best features of the American M1 and the German StG44. The Kalashnikov is a gas operated 7.62x39 assault rifle and by far the most prolific small arm of the 2nd half of the 20th century. It had been and still is, in more or less modified form, manufactured in dozens of countries and has been used in hundreds of conflicts since its introduction.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PBS-1 suppressor
 •  None due to the absence of a dovetail mount
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
en.st_wpn_akm_alfa_descr:
Developed in Israel by Command Arms & Acessories (CAA), the AK-Alfa assault rifle was first presented to the public in 2016. This weapon is marketed under two brands - "Kalashnikov USA" and "Kalashnikov Israel". Apparently, this weapon got into the Zone with the the ISG fuction. The AK-Alpha assault rifle is an attempt to improve the ergonomics of the AK rifle while maintaining its main advantages - high reliability and ease of maintenance. The base for the AK-Alpha is the receiver, the barrel with the gas tube, the bolt group and the main components of the trigger of the Russian-made Saiga carbine.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver except of scopes with very low mounting: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight.
  •  Silencer: 5.45/7.62x39 Hexagon Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_akm_bas_descr:
The AKM is a 7.62x39mm assault rifle designed by Mikhail Kalashnikov. It is an improved version of the AK-47 rifle and was developed in the 1950's. Introduced to the Red Army in 1959, the AKM is the most ubiquitous variant of the AK series of firearms and it has found widespread use with the former Warsaw Pact states and its many African and Asian allies. The production of AKM rifles was carried out at the Tula Arsenal and Izhevsk Mechanical Works.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: None
  •  Suppressor: PBS-1
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
en.st_wpn_akm_descr:
The AKM is a 7.62x39mm assault rifle designed by Mikhail Kalashnikov. It is an improved version of the AK-47 rifle and was developed in the 1950's. Introduced to the Red Army in 1959, the AKM is the most ubiquitous variant of the AK series of firearms and it has found widespread use with the former Warsaw Pact states and its many African and Asian allies. The production of AKM rifles was carried out at the Tula Arsenal and Izhevsk Mechanical Works.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PBS-1 suppressor
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
en.st_wpn_akm_isg_descr:
A custom model developed on the basis of AKM by the American company Rifle Dynamics. It is one of the most high-tech weapons in the Zone. Used by ISG special units.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ATPIAL (Advanced Target Pointer Illuminator Aiming Laser) AN/PEQ-15 produced by L3 Insight Technologies. Tactical device that combines laser designators in both visible and IR band with IR searchlight. 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver
  •  Silencer: Rifle Dynamics ISG Sound Suppressor
en.st_wpn_akms_bas_descr:
A variant of the AKM, equipped with an under-folding metal shoulder stock in place of the fixed wooden stock. The metal stock of the AKMS is somewhat different from the folding stock of the previous AK-47 model as it has a modified locking mechanism, which locks both support arms of the AKMS stock instead of just one.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: None
  •  PBS-1 suppressor
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
en.st_wpn_akms_descr:
A variant of the AKM, equipped with an under-folding metal shoulder stock in place of the fixed wooden stock. The metal stock of the AKMS is somewhat different from the folding stock of the previous AK-47 model as it has a modified locking mechanism, which locks both support arms of the AKMS stock instead of just one.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PBS-1 suppressor
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
en.st_wpn_aks74_descr:
A more accurate and reliable rifle than the AKM, the AK-74 is still used by the majority of countries of the former USSR. The AKS-74 is the foldable stock version of AK-74 and its stock has a triangular shape and lacks the folding shoulder pad of the AKMS and is folded to the left side of the receiver.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  TGP-A suppressor
en.st_wpn_aks_descr:
A variant of the AK-47, equipped with an under folding metal shoulder stock in place of the fixed wooden stock. It was designed for use inside armoured vehicles and by paratroopers.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PBS-1 suppressor
 •  None due to the absence of a dovetail mount
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (after modification)
en.st_wpn_aps_bas_apsabigo_descr:
The Stechkin APS is a Russian selective fire machine pistol, bearing the name of its designer Igor Stechkin. The Stechkin automatic pistol was originally chambered for 7.62x25mm Tokarev but was changed to the 9x18mm calibre used in the new Makarov pistol, as it became clear that this cartridge was set to become the new service ammunition for handguns in the Soviet Army. In 1951, both the Makarov and Stechkin were introduced into the Soviet military arsenal, replacing the ageing TT-33.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Installed Tactical Kit: Threaded barrel for using with APB sound suppressor, Tritium sights and ergonomic pistol grip rubber.
  •  Silencer: APB
en.st_wpn_aps_bas_descr:
The Stechkin APS is a Russian selective fire machine pistol, bearing the name of its designer Igor Stechkin. The Stechkin automatic pistol was originally chambered for 7.62x25mm Tokarev but was changed to the 9x18mm calibre used in the new Makarov pistol, as it became clear that this cartridge was set to become the new service ammunition for handguns in the Soviet Army. In 1951, both the Makarov and Stechkin were introduced into the Soviet military arsenal, replacing the ageing TT-33.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Can be upgraded using Tactical Kit.
  •  Silencer: None
en.st_wpn_aps_descr:
The Stechkin APS is a Russian selective fire machine pistol, bearing the name of its designer Igor Stechkin. The Stechkin automatic pistol was originally chambered for 7.62x25mm Tokarev but was changed to the 9x18mm calibre used in the new Makarov pistol, as it became clear that this cartridge was set to become the new service ammunition for handguns in the Soviet Army. In 1951, both the Makarov and Stechkin were introduced into the Soviet military arsenal, replacing the ageing TT-33.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  APS suppressor
en.st_wpn_ar15_freedom_descr:
A deeply modernized AR15 rifle, usually in service with the veterans of the Freedom. The body kit installed on the weapon allows you to feel confident at any firing range, but at the same time, the weapon is distinguished by a high price due to the use of very expensive components in the kit.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver (except of Red Dot Sights with low mount: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight and 1P76 Rakurs (picatinny).
  •  Installed Upgrades: B.E. MEYERS MAWL-C1 tactical green laser aim modul and Here Arms foregrip (helps in recoil control) and stock.
  •  Silencer: Surefire SOCOM556-MONSTER
en.st_wpn_ash12_descr:
The ASh-12.7 (АШ-12.7, which stands for "Автомат штурмовой 12.7мм" or "Automatic assault rifle, 12.7mm") battle rifle is a dedicated CQB/Urban Operations weapon, developed by TsKIB SOO ("Central Design and Research Bureau of Sporting and Hunting Arms"), a subsidiary of the KBP Instrument Design Bureau of Tula, Russia, by request from the Russian FSB (Federal Security Service). The weapon was designed with extreme short-range stopping power in mind because it is intended for special units of the FSB which operate in urban environments against heavily armed and organized gangsters and terrorists who need to be killed in one shot. According to the Russian press, the first batch of ASh-12.7 assault rifles was delivered to the FSB.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, C-more Railway Tactical, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_aug_a1_bas_descr:
Steyr AUG A1 in standard rifle configuration (military green colour). The AUG is an Austrian bullpup 5.56mm assault rifle, designed in the 1960s by Steyr Mannlicher GmbH & Co KG (formerly Steyr-Daimler-Puch). The AUG (Armee-Universal-Gewehr "universal army rifle") was adopted by the Austrian Army as the StG 77 (Sturmgewehr 77) in 1978, where it replaced the 7.62mm StG 58 automatic rifle (a licence-built FN FAL). In production since 1978, it is the standard small arm of the Austrian Bundesheer and various national police units.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  1.5x scope (integrated).
  •  Suppressor: Steyr UMS
en.st_wpn_aug_a1_custom_bas_descr:
Steyr AUG A1 in standard rifle configuration (military green colour). The AUG is an Austrian bullpup 5.56mm assault rifle, designed in the 1960s by Steyr Mannlicher GmbH&Co KG (formerly Steyr-Daimler-Puch). The AUG (Armee-Universal-Gewehr "universal army rifle") was adopted by the Austrian Army as the StG 77 (Sturmgewehr 77) in 1978, where it replaced the 7.62mm StG 58 automatic rifle (a licence-built FN FAL). In production since 1978, it is the standard small arm of the Austrian Bundesheer and various national police units. CQB - has less weight and increased rate of fire compared to the original.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  1.5x scope (integrated).
  •  Suppressor: Steyr UMS
en.st_wpn_aug_a3_bas_descr:
Steyr AUG (Armee Universal Gewehr - Army Universal Rifle) is an Austrian 5.56x45mm NATO bullpup assault rifle, designed in the 1960s by Steyr-Daimler-Puch and now manufactured by Steyr Mannlicher GmbH & Co KG. The Steyr AUG A3 differs from the base model in the presence of four Picatinny slats: the top one is used for attaching sights, a grenade launcher or a vertical front handle can be attached to the bottom, and a laser pointer or other necessary additional equipment is attached to the side.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All for picatinny/weaver except of scopes with very low mounting: Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Docter Sight.
  •  Suppressor: Steyr UMS
en.st_wpn_aug_a3_custom_bas_descr:
Steyr AUG (Armee Universal Gewehr - Army Universal Rifle) is an Austrian 5.56x45mm NATO bullpup assault rifle, designed in the 1960s by Steyr-Daimler-Puch and now manufactured by Steyr Mannlicher GmbH & Co KG. The Steyr AUG A3 differs from the base model in the presence of four Picatinny slats: the top one is used for attaching sights, a grenade launcher or a vertical front handle can be attached to the bottom, and a laser pointer or other necessary additional equipment is attached to the side.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Installed Trijicon Acog (x4) and PEQ LA5 LAM.
  •  Suppressor: Steyr UMS
en.st_wpn_aug_a3_descr:
Steyr AUG (Armee Universal Gewehr - Army Universal Rifle) is an Austrian 5.56x45mm NATO bullpup assault rifle, designed in the 1960s by Steyr-Daimler-Puch and now manufactured by Steyr Mannlicher GmbH & Co KG. The Steyr AUG A3 differs from the base model in the presence of four Picatinny slats: the top one is used for attaching sights, a grenade launcher or a vertical front handle can be attached to the bottom, and a laser pointer or other necessary additional equipment is attached to the side.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, C-more Railway Tactical, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_aug_descr:
Steyr AUG A1 in standard rifle configuration (military green colour). The AUG is an Austrian bullpup 5.56mm assault rifle, designed in the 1960s by Steyr Mannlicher GmbH & Co KG (formerly Steyr-Daimler-Puch). The AUG (Armee-Universal-Gewehr "universal army rifle") was adopted by the Austrian Army as the StG 77 (Sturmgewehr 77) in 1978, where it replaced the 7.62mm StG 58 automatic rifle (a licence-built FN FAL). In production since 1978, it is the standard small arm of the Austrian Bundesheer and various national police units.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  1.5x scope (integrated).
en.st_wpn_aug_modern_descr:
Steyr AUG A1 in standard rifle configuration (military green colour). The AUG is an Austrian bullpup 5.56mm assault rifle, designed in the 1960s by Steyr Mannlicher GmbH & Co KG (formerly Steyr-Daimler-Puch). The AUG (Armee-Universal-Gewehr "universal army rifle") was adopted by the Austrian Army as the StG 77 (Sturmgewehr 77) in 1978, where it replaced the 7.62mm StG 58 automatic rifle (a licence-built FN FAL). In production since 1978, it is the standard small arm of the Austrian Bundesheer and various national police units. This model has been modified for usage of 7.62x51 NATO cartridge.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  1.5x scope (integrated).
en.st_wpn_beretta_descr:
The 92F is perhaps best known as the standard pistol of the US Military, replacing the legendary M1911. It is a short-recoil operated, locked-breech weapon with an aluminium frame and a hard chromed barrel bore. A popular sidearm adopted by several countries, it is especially popular among criminals for its high-powered rounds and high-capacity magazine.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  9x19 compatible suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_bizon_descr:
This submachine gun has been developed in Izhevsk at the famous IZHMASH state arms factory during the early 1990s, first appearing to the public in 1993. Influenced by the Calico submachine guns, Bizon (Bison) SMG combined high capacity helical magazine with more conventional appearance and layout. The Bizon is built on shortened AKS-74 receivers, made from stamped steel. It featured a simple blowback action, hammer fired from closed bolt. The Bizon is capable of semi-automatic and automatic fire, with typical AK-type safety - selector lever on the right side of the receiver. Sights are similar to ones found on the AKS-74U, with hooded front post and flip up rear.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EKP-8-02 Kobra, 1P29, PSO-1 scopes
  •  PBS-1 suppressor
en.st_wpn_bm16_descr: A double-barrelled shotgun with shortened barrels. Lighter and more compact, effective at close range. Needing a cheap, short range weapon that's effective and lethal, many rookie stalkers resort to sawing off the buttstock and barrel on the easily acquired TOZ 66. This makes the shotgun quick to wield, but at the cost of range and accuracy.\n \n
en.st_wpn_bm16_full_descr: A powerful weapon that every stalker can afford, TOZ 66 is a close range firearm possessing both reliability and effectiveness. However, being large and unwieldy, many stalkers prefer to spend their hard-earned rubles on a modern handgun instead.\n \n
en.st_wpn_colt1911_camo_descr:
The M1911 was the standard-issue side arm for the US military from 1911 to 1985. An extremely reliable and robust weapon, it's been adopted for military, police and civilian use around the world and remains a very popular firearm to this day. This is a unique model with red stains on dark-beige colour.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  .45 ACP suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_colt1911_descr:
The M1911 was the standard-issue side arm for the US military from 1911 to 1985. An extremely reliable and robust weapon, it's been adopted for military, police and civilian use around the world and remains a very popular firearm to this day.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  .45 ACP suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_colt1911_duty_descr:
The M1911 was the standard-issue side arm for the US military from 1911 to 1985. An extremely reliable and robust weapon, it's been adopted for military, police and civilian use around the world and remains a very popular firearm to this day. This variant has a distinctive feature - it's more powerful than the normal one.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  .45 ACP suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_colt1911_merc_descr:
The M1911 was the standard-issue side arm for the US military from 1911 to 1985. An extremely reliable and robust weapon, it's been adopted for military, police and civilian use around the world and remains a very popular firearm to this day. This model is considerably more reliable and accurate.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  .45 ACP suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_colt1911_n_descr: An improved version of a classic automatic pistol, which passed all the major armed conflicts of the 20th century with flying colours and entered the new millennium with confidence. Produced by dozens if not hundreds of arms manufacturers around the world in both standard and modified configurations. The weapon's only drawback, its mediocre magazine capacity, is largely compensated by the hard-hitting ammo it uses.\n
en.st_wpn_cz75_descr:
The CZ-75 was developed in the Czech Republic by the Koucky brothers. This semi-automatic pistol first appeared in 1975, entered production in 1976 and was intended for the export market. The CZ-75 accumulated the best features from many preceding designs and the resulting pistol was aesthetically attractive, comfortable to handle, shoot, accurate and reliable in operation. Made by CZ arms in various versions and modifications, the CZ-75 was also widely copied and cloned.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  9x19 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_desert_eagle_descr: The IMI Desert Eagle is a semi-automatic handgun notable for chambering the largest centrefire cartridge of any magazine fed, self-loading pistol and also being the heaviest and blockiest gun of its type. No one knows why IMI thought shooting a calibre of this size out of a handgun was a good idea, even though there are plenty of rounds out there that can do the job without breaking your wrist; this one assumes because of a pissing contest between the .44 and .50 AE. Not only is it heavy and harsh in recoil, you can also use it to bludgeon your enemies to death because of its blockiness and weight. Although militaries and law enforcement around the world, in their moment of sanity, never adopted this brick; Hollywood did, mostly because of its "cool factor" and now is the poster child for gangsters and pimps and a status symbol for those who reached a certain rank in a certain console shooter. Rumour is that there's a stalker with a crowbar wandering around the Zone with a rechambered version of this gun, but it's just a rumour.
en.st_wpn_desert_eagle_nimble_descr:
The IMI Desert Eagle is a semi-automatic handgun notable for chambering the largest centrefire cartridge of any magazine fed, self-loading pistol and also being the heaviest and blockiest gun of its type. No one knows why IMI thought shooting a calibre of this size out of a handgun was a good idea, even though there are plenty of rounds out there that can do the job without breaking your wrist; this one assumes because of a pissing contest between the .44 and .50 AE. This weapon was brought to the Zone by a stalker named Logan Williams. Disguised as a TIME photographer he managed to single-handedly enter the centre of Pripyat. His current whereabouts are unknown, but he is rumoured to work as an information broker specializing in all matters related to the Zone.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  DOCTER sight (integrated)
en.st_wpn_dvl10_descr:
DVL-10 is a lightweight and compact sniper rifle in proprietary and current military subsonic calibers has been developed for special service and combat missions where exceptional accuracy and range are needed.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver except of red dot sights.
  •  Silencer: None
en.st_wpn_dvl10_m1_descr:
DVL-10 is a lightweight, compact, and silenced rifle in proprietary and current military subsonic calibers and has been developed for special service and combat missions where exceptional accuracy and range are needed.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver except of red dot sights.
  •  Silencer: Integrated
en.st_wpn_eft_rsh12_descr: The powerful RSh-12 (Revolver Shturmovoy 12 - "Assault Revolver 12") revolver, manufactured by KBP Instrument Design Bureau, designed for use by special forces. Despite the powerful 12.7x55 mm caliber, RSh-12 has less recoil than many other large-caliber revolvers and pistols, since the shot is fired from the bottom chamber of the cylinder. \n \n
en.st_wpn_fal_aus_descr:
SA-58 manufactured by american company DSA (David Selveggio Arms) is a legal copy of the FAL, it's made with the same Steyr-Daimler-Puch production line equipment as the StG-58. This modelel comes with 21" barrel length, and other "Austrian Style" stuff like original Austrian foregrip for a FAL, aluminum-alloy lower receiver, and improved Glass-filled Nylon furniture. This model used by military and Law Enforcement and can run a fully automatic. The DSA-58 can use any metric-measurement FAL magazines.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: None 
 
  •  Can be upgraded using Tactical Kits 
 
  •  Silencer: AAC 7.62 SDN-6
en.st_wpn_fal_descr:
FN FAL is a NATO firearm produced in Belgium by Fabrique Nationale de Herstal. One of the most recognized and popular automatic rifles. The FN FAL was originally developed for an intermediate 7.92x33 mm cartridge used by the Germans during World War II, then prototypes for the .280 British cartridge appeared. It was later remade chambered for 7.62x51 mm NATO, adopted as a single patron for NATO countries. During the Cold War, it received the nickname "the right hand of the free world."
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, C-more Railway Tactical, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_fal_sa58_osw_descr:
SA-58 OSW (Operations Specialist Weapon) manufactured by American company DSA (David Selvaggio Arms) is a legal copy of the FAL, its made with the same Steyr-Daimler-Puch production line equipment as the StG-58. It may come with a different barrel length, an aluminum-alloy lower receiver, and improved Glass-filled Nylon furniture. Civilian models are semi-automatic, but military and Law Enforcement clients can procure select-fire models that have a fully automatic cyclic rate of 750 rounds/minute. The DSA-58 can use any metric-measurement FAL magazines.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: All scopes for picatinny/weaver
  •  Silencer: AAC 7.62 SDN-6
en.st_wpn_famas3_descr:
FAMAS stands for Fusil d'Assaut de la Manufacture d'Armes de St-Etienne (Assault Rifle by St-Etienne Arms Factory). Development of this rifle began in 1967, by Paul Tellie, a French arms designer. This new rifle was intended to replace the ageing MAS Mle.49/56 semi-automatic rifles, MAT-49 submachine guns and MAC Mle.1929 light machine guns. The first FAMAS prototype had been built in 1971, and French military began to test this rifle in 1972. Meanwhile, France adopted a Swiss-designed SIG SG-540 5.56mm assault rifle as an intermediate solution. The FAMAS was adopted by the French in 1978 and since then became a standard French Army rifle, known among the French soldiers as "Le Clairon" (the bulge).
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_fn2000_descr:
From the late 1980s to early 1990s, FN Herstal began to develop a new rifle. The aim was to produce a modern and modular weapon resulting in the FN F2000 rifle, first displayed to the public in 2001. The F2000 offers all of the popular features of the modern assault rifle such as a compact bullpup layout, ambidextrous handling and a modular design with plenty of options and addons available. So far, the FN F2000 rifle has found only a few buyers, including the Armed Forces of Slovenia and Belgian Special Operations Forces.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  FN2000 scope (integrated)
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_fn2000_nimble_descr:
From the late 1980s to early 1990s, FN Herstal began to develop a new rifle. The aim was to produce a modern and modular weapon resulting in the FN F2000 rifle, first displayed to the public in 2001. The F2000 offers all of the popular features of the modern assault rifle such as a compact bullpup layout, ambidextrous handling and a modular design with plenty of options and addons available. So far, the FN F2000 rifle has found only a few buyers, including the Armed Forces of Slovenia and Belgian Special Operations Forces. This model has an integrated EOTech scope, which results in slightly lower accuracy, but since this model was aimed for CQC, rate of fire was increased accordingly.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  EOTech sight (integrated)
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_fn57_bas_descr:
The Five-seveN is a delayed blowback operated, semi-automatic firearm. The Five-seveN pistol was designed by FN Herstal as a complimentary sidearm for the P90 submachine gun. The P90 and Five-seveN share the same ammunition, the 5.7x28mm SS190 cartridge. The idea behind the ammunition and weapon is to provide good penetration against targets protected by body armour, while keeping the weapon's weight, dimensions and recoil at reasonable levels.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  The kit includes Laser Aiming Module and Red Dot Sights mount
  •  Scopes: Docter Sight, Aimpoint T1 (low mount), Trijicon RMR, Leupold DeltaPoint PRO.
  •  Silencer: 5.7x28 suppressor
en.st_wpn_fn57_descr: The Five-seveN is a delayed blowback operated, semi-automatic firearm. The Five-seveN pistol was designed by FN Herstal as a complimentary sidearm for the P90 submachine gun. The P90 and Five-seveN share the same ammunition, the 5.7x28mm SS190 cartridge. The idea behind the ammunition and weapon is to provide good penetration against targets protected by body armour, while keeping the weapon's weight, dimensions and recoil at reasonable levels.
en.st_wpn_fnc_descr:
The FNC (Fabrique Nationale Carabine) is a 5.56x45mm NATO assault rifle developed by the Belgian arms manufacturer FN Herstal and introduced in the late 1970s.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_fnp45_descr:
The FN FNP pistol is a series of semi-automatic, polymer framed pistols manufactured in Columbia, South Carolina by FNH USA, a division of Fabrique Nationale de Herstal. Debuted in early 2006, the FNP series weapons are hammer fired weapons utilizing a Browning cam system with an external extractor. The trigger module is housed inside the polymer frame as an individual unit connected to the hammer. The magazine release button is held in place by a retention pin which can be removed to allow the button to be reversed.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GemTech suppressor
en.st_wpn_fnx45_descr:
The FNX line of pistols were introduced in 2009 by FNH USA, a US based subsidiary of the Belgian gun company FN Herstal. Originally developed to the requirements of the US Joint Combat Pistol Program, these pistols are manufactured in US and intended for military, law enforcement and civilian use. All FNX handguns are semi-automatic pistols with double/single action trigger and external hammer. Frame of the every FNX pistol is made from impact resistant polymer, with removable backstraps of different sizes. FNX-45 Tactical is a specialised variant of the basic FNX-45, that features extended threaded barrel and raised sights, to be used with detachable sound suppressor.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  FNX Tactical sight (integrated)
  •  GemTech suppressor (integrated)
en.st_wpn_fort500_descr: The Fort-500 smoothbore rifle was developed by the Fort NGO of the Ministry of Internal Affairs of Ukraine in the late 1990s, and was intended to arm the staff of the Ministry of Internal Affairs and security services of Ukraine. At present, the FORT-500 rifle in limited quantities is in service with the special units of the Ministry of Internal Affairs of Ukraine, and is also sold to citizens of this country in a hunting variant.\n \n
en.st_wpn_fort_descr:
The Fort-17 pistol is the latest development of the Ukrainian state owned FORT factory. It is a polymer framed modification of the earlier Fort-12 pistol, and all the mechanisms and properties are almost identical between the two pistols. However, the Fort-17 is lighter than its predecessor and features a removable grip backstrap, which can be had in various sizes, to adapt the pistol for a particular shooter.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  9x19 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_g28_descr:
The HK G28 rifle was developed by Heckler&Koch specifically for the Bundeswehr as a support weapon for small infantry units. The G28 is based on the HK MR308, which, in turn, is a civilian version of the HK 417 automatic rifle. Despite several differences, the HK G28 is 75% interchangeable with the HK 417. This rifle is designed to shoot at distances inaccessible to standard 5.56mm assault rifles.
 The Schmidt & Bender PM II 3-12x50 riflescope was originally created for elite military forces for high-quality target acquisition and pinpoint accuracy. The scope is outfitted with an illuminate Tremor2 reticle that offers a variety of markings to calculate precise shots while taking distance and windage into account.
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: Schmidt&Bender PM II 3-12x50 scope and Aimpoint T1 included.
  •  Suppressor: HK G28 B&T QD 7.62x51 sound suppressor.
en.st_wpn_g36_descr:
The H&K G-36 was known as the HK-50 project in early 1990s. After the cancellation of the G11 and G41 projects, the Bundeswehr was left with outdated G3 rifles; Heckler & Koch was set to develop a new assault rifle for both the German army and for export. The new 5.56x45mm assault rifle was adopted by the Bundeswehr in 1995 and also found its way into the hands of various law enforcement agencies worldwide, including British police and some US police departments. So far, there have been very few complaints about this rifle. The more common ones being the overheating of the handguards during sustained fire, and the loss of zero on the built in scope of G36KE rifles. Some German soldiers also complained about the position of the dual optical sights and it being easily fogged in bad weather. Otherwise, it is accurate, reliable and simple in maintenance.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_g36k_descr:
A shortened variant of the G-36 rifle with a shorter barrel, fitted with an open type flash suppressor, a shorter forend and a bottom rail that can be used to attach tactical accessories, such as a UTL flashlight. The rifle's barrel lacks the ability to launch rifle grenades and will not support a bayonet. G36K in service with German special forces are issued with a 100 round C-Mag drum. There are two variants of the G-36K, first has 3x scope with carry handle attached to the top and the second is fitted with iron sights and rail.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  G36 scope (integrated)
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
en.st_wpn_g3_descr:
The G3A3 is a battle rifle developed in the 1950s by the German arms manufacturer Heckler & Koch GmbH in collaboration with the Spanish state owned design and development agency CETME (Centro de Estudios Tecnicos de Materiales Especiales). It is designed to use the 7.62x51mm NATO round, and uses a Roller-delayed blowback action. The rifle owes its design to the CETME mod. A, which was designed by the german engineer Dr. Ludwig Vorgrimler. In 1957, Heckler & Koch bought the license to manufacture the rifle, where small modifications were made to the CETME design. Officially adopted into service in 1959, by the Bundeswehr as the G3, it remained in service until 1995 when it was replaced by the G36. Despite having been officially replaced by the German army, it is still in limited use.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  5.56x45 compatible suppressor
  •  ACOG, Barska ES AC10632, EOTech scopes
en.st_wpn_g3sg1_descr:
A sniper variant of the G3. The "SG" stands for Scharfschutzengewehr, or "sharpshooting rifle". The rifles were individually selected from the production line for their accuracy and then modified. A Zeiss 1.5-6x variable power telescopic sight was added using a HK claw mount attached to the receiver. The stock was extended slightly and comes with a heavy, dual-stage buffer and adjustable cheek rest. A special set trigger group was added for a crisp trigger pull of 1 lb. Automatic fire was retained.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  G3 scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_g43_descr:
Adopting a simple gas-operated mechanism from Soviet SVT rifles, Nazi Germany developed the Gewehr 43 semi-automatic rifle. It was renamed Karabiner 43 in 1944. Equipped with the ZF 4 telescopic sight, it is an effective sniper rifle.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  ZF-41 scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_galil_descr:
Formally accepted into service with the IDF in 1972, the IMI Galil is designed for operation in arid conditions and is based on the Finnish RK 62, which itself was derived from the Soviet AK-47. The Galil is noted for its reliability in combat conditions, and ease of maintenance. It has a line chambered for 5.56x45mm and one for 7.62x51mm.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PBS-1 suppressor
  •  Galil 4x scope (after modification)
en.st_wpn_gauss_bas_descr:
A highly experimental, artefact-powered, super-accurate weapon made by an unknown party in the Zone. Its gunshot is nearly inaudible, because instead of gunpowder, it uses magnets to accelerate its bullet to incredible speeds. Despite the gauss rifle's tremendous firepower, the recoil is insignificant. The accuracy and power of this gun exceeds those of all other small arms.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Enhanced computer EOTS-62 scope (integrated)
en.st_wpn_glock17_descr:
The Glock 17 is a polymer-framed, short recoil-operated, locked-breech semi-automatic pistol designed and produced by Austrian manufacturer Glock Ges.m.b.H.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: None
  •  Silencer: None
en.st_wpn_glock17_m1_descr:
The Glock 17 is a polymer-framed, short recoil-operated, locked-breech semi-automatic pistol designed and produced by Austrian manufacturer Glock Ges.m.b.H. The tactical variant of Glock 17 has some additional futures: integrated Aimtech glock base for Red Dot sights, installed Pachmayr tactical rubber grip and threaded barrel manufactured by Double Diamond.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  Scopes: Docter Sight, Aimpoint T1 (low mount only), Trijicon RMR, Leupold Deltapoint PRO
  •  Silencer: Osprey 9mm
en.st_wpn_glock_descr:
The Glock 19 is a smaller version of the Glock 17. Its reduced size made the Glock 19 easier to conceal and carry by military and police forces. The pistol was debuted by Glock GbmH of Austria in 1988. When compared to the Glock 17, the Glock 19 retains the weight of its slide to keep the short recoil operation in check while at the same time shortening the grip and barrel. Many components of the Glock 19 were made interchangeable with the Glock 17, including the magazine. Simply put, the Glock 19 is a compact version of the 17.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GemTech suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_glock_modern_descr:
The Glock 19 is a smaller version of the Glock 17. Its reduced size made the Glock 19 easier to conceal and carry by military and police forces. The pistol was debuted by Glock GbmH of Austria in 1988. When compared to the Glock 17, the Glock 19 retains the weight of its slide to keep the short recoil operation in check while at the same time shortening the grip and barrel. Many components of the Glock 19 were made interchangeable with the Glock 17, including the magazine. Simply put, the Glock 19 is a compact version of the 17. This model has an optional automatic fire mode.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  GemTech suppressor (after modification)
en.st_wpn_grach_descr: The MP-443 is a short recoil operated, locked breech pistol that was developed for the Russian Army trials, codenamed "Grach" (rook). These trials were started by issuing initial requirements for a new military pistol in 1993. Several designs were submitted to the trials and the winner was the pistol designed at the state Izhevsk Mechanical Plant by Vladimir Yarygin. This pistol, initially known as the 6P35 military index during trials or the MP-443 "Grach" manufacturer's designation, is adopted by the Russian Government for military and law enforcement use under the PYa designation, and already issued to some Russian Army units.
en.st_wpn_groza_descr:
The OTs-14 "Groza" ("Thunder") modular assault rifle was developed during the early 1990s at the TSKIB SOO. It was intended for various special forces in the Russian Army and Internal Affairs Ministry as an dedicated urban warfare weapon. Briefly manufactured in small numbers at the Tula Arms factory during the mid-1990s, the OTs-14 saw action in Chechnya in 1999, but fell out of favour and are no longer being made. It is a bullpup design, with removable foregrip which can be replaced with a 40mm grenade launcher. Adopted for Spetsnaz forces and specialist front-line combat units, the OTs-14-4A allows for assembly of one of four weapon versions, depending on the mission. This Zone version is equipped with the GP-25 grenade launcher. 
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PO 4x34 scope
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (integrated)
en.st_wpn_groza_nimble_descr:
Being the only variant of the OTs-14-4A, the OTs-14-1A "Groza-1" is chambered for 7.62x39mm. Fire rate, flatness and magazine capacity are improved thanks to the use of this lighter, faster round. The Groza-1 was only produced in small batches.
 
 ATTACHMENTS: 
  •  PO4x34 scope
  •  GP-25 grenade launcher (integrated)
en.st_wpn_gsh18_descr: The GSh-18 is a 9x19mm semi-automatic pistol developed in the early 1990s at the KBP Instrument Design Bureau in Tula. The pistol's name is derived from its designers - Gryazev and Shipunow, and the number 18 denotes the magazine capacity. The GSh-18 is a recoil-operated, locked breech pistol. The weapon has an unusual cam-rotated barrel with 10 locking lugs. The GSh-18 is striker fired and features a pre-set trigger that pre-cocks the pistol when the slide recoils after firing a shot, the striker firing pin is then fully cocked by pulling the trigger back to the rear.
